schoolName: EDU - Digital Education Holdings (EDU Medical, Faculty of Medicine)

sources.label: EDU official site (Wayback Machine capture) - Programme in Medicine (B.Med./M.Med.) overview
sources.notes: Live medical.edu.mt was unreachable during this research pass (network timeout on port 443), so the latest available archived capture of the official page was used. Confirms: 5.5-year Programme in Medicine = 3-year Bachelor of Medicine (B.Med., 180 ECTS) + 2.5-year Master of Medicine (M.Med., 162 ECTS); blended model with theoretical study on EDU's digital campus and 4-week clinical rotation blocks at partner teaching hospitals in Germany (Helios Kliniken, Marienhaus-Gruppe and others); clinical experience begins from Week 10 of the Bachelor; Master concludes with a 6-month Clinical Clerkship; registered operator address given as SOHO The Strand, Fawwara Building, Triq L-Imsida, Gzira - GZR 1401, Malta (DEH Reg. No. C 82123) as printed in this 2024-dated capture's footer -- superseded by the more current Euroguidance Malta directory address used for this draft (see below).

sources.label: EDU official site (Wayback Machine capture) - Admissions (application process, prerequisites)
sources.notes: Confirms 5-step admissions process (online application, ITB Consulting online cognitive test, video interview, admission decision, matriculation); prerequisite documents include a secondary/matriculation certificate (Abitur/IB/A-Level or equivalent), police check, CV, cover letter, valid passport/ID, English B2 proficiency, and -- materially -- a German language certificate at C1 level for clinical training, since partner teaching hospitals operate in German (waived only if the applicant's prior formal education was conducted in German).

sources.label: EDU official site (Wayback Machine capture) - EU-wide Accreditation and Recognition
sources.notes: Confirms: EDU (via Digital Education Holdings Ltd) accredited 28 June 2018 as an Institute of Higher Education under the Maltese Education Act, licence 2018-10, originally by NCFHE, now the Malta Further and Higher Education Authority (MFHEA); B.Med. and M.Med. additionally accredited by AHPGS (a German Akkreditierungsrat-approved agency) on 4 May 2023; since February 2020 the Medicine programme operates under Article 24 (EU Directive 2005/36/EC) supervision by the Faculty of Health, Medicine and Life Sciences (FHML) at Maastricht University; page explicitly states the Medical Council of Malta is the competent authority for automatic EU recognition and that, at time of that capture, EDU was still 'in discussions' with the Medical Council to secure that automatic recognition -- i.e. full automatic EU recognition was not yet confirmed as finalised. Page also states EDU expected its first graduates in 2025, confirming this is a young, not-yet-track-recorded programme for outbound graduates.

sources.label: Euroguidance Malta (Ministry-linked national directory) - EDU / Digital Education Holdings Ltd listing
sources.notes: Independent, currently-live government-linked directory listing. Confirms current registered address and locality as 'Chaplain's House, Villa Bighi, Marina Street, Kalkara KKR 1320, Malta' (locality filter: Kalkara), phone (+356) 22 263 120, sector 'Sciences / Health Care', founded June 2018; describes EDU Medical as Europe's first digital medical school. This is used as the corroborated current city/locality for this draft in preference to the older Gzira address seen in the 2024 Wayback capture of EDU's own footer, since the Euroguidance listing is independently maintained and more recent at time of this check.

sources.label: World Directory of Medical Schools (WDOMS/FAIMER) - search spot-checks
sources.notes: Spot-checked several WDOMS Malta-linked FAIMER school IDs surfaced in web search results (F0005150, F0009016, F0000867, F0006390, F0002339, F0002675); none corresponds to EDU / Digital Education Holdings Ltd (F0005150 is Queen Mary University of London / Barts Malta on Gozo; F0009016 is ASOMI College of Sciences; the rest are unrelated schools in other countries). No WDOMS listing for EDU / Digital Education Holdings was located during this research pass. This is treated as 'not confirmed listed' rather than 'confirmed absent', and the page must instruct students to verify current WDOMS status themselves rather than asserting a listing exists.

sources.label: Third-party study-abroad aggregator - future-doctor.eu Malta medical-study guide (EDU fee estimate)
sources.notes: Independent secondary source estimating EDU's tuition at approximately EUR 19,500 per year, and separately noting Helios clinics in Wiesbaden, Gotha and Hildesheim, Germany as partner clinical-training sites. This figure is a third-party estimate, not sourced from EDU's own current published fee schedule (which was not directly retrievable during this pass since medical.edu.mt was unreachable live and no archived tuition-specific page was found), so it is presented as an indicative range attributed to this source rather than an official confirmed figure.

sources.label: Wikipedia - Kalkara
sources.notes: Independent corroboration of city/context facts: Kalkara is a small village (~3,000-3,200 residents) on the eastern shore of Malta's Grand Harbour, adjoining the historic 'Three Cities' (Cottonera) area, about 4km from Valletta and roughly 3km from Malta International Airport; quiet, low-key, non-touristy character relative to the Three Cities and to nightlife hubs like St Julian's/Sliema; home to Fort Ricasoli and Malta's national science centre.

structuredFacts.name: EDU - Digital Education Holdings (EDU Medical, Faculty of Medicine)
bestFitFor: Students who are comfortable with a primarily digital/online theoretical-learning model rather than a traditional in-person lecture-hall medical school, and who are self-motivated in a small-group, tutor-mentored format

Students who can realistically commit to reaching German C1 language proficiency (or already have German-medium schooling), since EDU's partner clinical teaching hospitals operate in Germany and clinical rotations are conducted in German

Students prepared to independently verify -- before enrolling -- EDU's current WDOMS listing status, current Medical Council of Malta automatic-recognition status, and India's NMC/NEET/FMGE-NExT requirements, since none of these is asserted as settled on this page

Students drawn to a newer, EU-accredited (MFHEA-licensed, AHPGS-accredited) medical programme supervised under EU Directive 2005/36/EC by Maastricht University's Faculty of Health, Medicine and Life Sciences, rather than a centuries-old traditional faculty

Students who value programme flexibility/portability (location-independent theoretical study) over a single fixed home campus, and who are comfortable being among the programme's earliest graduating cohorts (first graduates expected 2025)

programs.title: Programme in Medicine: Bachelor of Medicine (B.Med.) + Master of Medicine (M.Med.)
programs.medium: English for theoretical/online coursework; German for clinical rotations at partner teaching hospitals (a German C1 language certificate is a stated admissions prerequisite for clinical training, waivable only if the applicant's own prior schooling was conducted in German)
programs.notes: Structured as a 3-year Bachelor of Medicine (B.Med., MQF/EQF Level 6, 180 ECTS) followed directly by a 2.5-year Master of Medicine (M.Med., MQF/EQF Level 7, 162 ECTS), together forming a combined 5.5-year, approximately 342-ECTS Programme in Medicine. Delivery is a blended model: theoretical content is taught via EDU's own online 'digital campus' platform (synchronous small-group, problem-based learning in groups of up to 5, with dedicated tutors/mentors), while practical clinical experience takes place in 4-week rotation blocks at EDU's partner teaching hospitals, which are based in Germany (named partners include Helios Kliniken and Marienhaus-Gruppe); clinical exposure begins from around Week 10 of the Bachelor, and the Master concludes with a 6-month Clinical Clerkship. EDU is licensed as an Institution of Higher Education under the Maltese Education Act (licence 2018-10) by the Malta Further and Higher Education Authority (MFHEA), and the B.Med./M.Med. programmes are additionally accredited by AHPGS, a German Akkreditierungsrat-approved agency, as of 4 May 2023. Since February 2020 the programme has operated under Article 24 (EU Directive 2005/36/EC) external supervision by the Faculty of Health, Medicine and Life Sciences (FHML) at Maastricht University. As of the most recent archived official accreditation page reviewed for this research, EDU stated it was still 'in discussions' with the Medical Council of Malta to secure automatic EU recognition of the degree -- this had not yet been confirmed as finalised at that point, and EDU expected its first graduates only in 2025, meaning outbound recognition track record is very limited. No WDOMS (World Directory of Medical Schools) listing for EDU/Digital Education Holdings could be located during this research pass. Admission requires a matriculation-level secondary certificate (Abitur/IB/A-Level or equivalent), an ITB Consulting online cognitive test, a video interview, English B2 proficiency, and a German C1 language certificate for clinical training. Indian applicants must independently verify current NMC recognition status, NEET eligibility, and FMGE/NExT licensing requirements with Indian regulators, and current WDOMS/automatic-EU-recognition status directly with EDU and the Medical Council of Malta, before enrolling -- this page does not assert any of these as confirmed.

structuredFacts.researchNotes: EDU is the consumer/brand name for Digital Education Holdings Ltd (DEH), a private Maltese Institution of Higher Education licensed by the Malta Further and Higher Education Authority (MFHEA, licence 2018-10, originally granted by NCFHE in June/2018). It operates Europe's first primarily digital medical school: a combined 5.5-year Programme in Medicine (3-year B.Med. + 2.5-year M.Med., ~342 ECTS) in which theoretical study happens online via EDU's own 'digital campus' and clinical rotations happen in 4-week blocks at partner teaching hospitals in Germany (named partners: Helios Kliniken, Marienhaus-Gruppe). This is a materially different delivery model from a traditional in-person medical school and must be described plainly and accurately rather than framed as a conventional campus-based MBBS. Since February 2020 the programme has been supervised under Article 24 of EU Directive 2005/36/EC by the Faculty of Health, Medicine and Life Sciences (FHML) at Maastricht University, and the B.Med./M.Med. were additionally accredited by the German agency AHPGS on 4 May 2023 -- both genuine, verifiable quality-assurance facts. However, as of the most recent archived official EDU accreditation page reviewed, EDU was still 'in discussions' with the Medical Council of Malta to secure automatic EU recognition of the degree (not yet confirmed finalised at that point), and EDU expected its first graduates only in 2025 -- meaning there is very limited real-world outbound-recognition track record to date. No listing for EDU/Digital Education Holdings could be found in the World Directory of Medical Schools (WDOMS) during this research pass (several superficially similar Malta-linked WDOMS IDs surfaced in search were checked and confirmed to be different schools, e.g. Queen Mary University of London/Barts Malta and ASOMI College of Sciences). Because WDOMS listing is generally part of the pathway India's NMC uses to assess foreign medical qualifications, and because this page found no confirmed WDOMS entry for EDU, the page must not assert NMC/NEET/FMGE recognition and must instruct students to verify current WDOMS status, Medical Council of Malta recognition status, and NMC status directly before enrolling. A further material, easily overlooked caveat for Indian applicants: clinical rotations are conducted in German at partner hospitals in Germany, and EDU's own admissions prerequisites list a German C1 language certificate as a requirement for clinical training (waived only if prior schooling was in German) -- alongside English B2 for the online coursework. This is a substantive extra language burden beyond typical English-medium MBBS-abroad programmes and must not be downplayed. On location: EDU's own 2024-dated Wayback-archived page footer lists a Gzira address (SOHO The Strand, Fawwara Building, Triq L-Imsida, Gzira), while the independently maintained Euroguidance Malta directory (government-linked, checked live during this research pass) lists a different, apparently more current address and locality: Chaplain's House, Villa Bighi, Marina Street, Kalkara KKR 1320, phone (+356) 22 263 120. This draft uses Kalkara as the corroborated current locality since the Euroguidance listing was checked live and is independently maintained, while noting the discrepancy for transparency; students should confirm EDU's current operating address directly. Given EDU's digital-first model, students should also understand day-to-day 'campus life' is centred on the online platform rather than a single traditional student-town campus, so city-profile framing here describes Kalkara/Malta as the country context and registered base rather than an intensive daily-commute campus town. Tuition: EDU's own current fee schedule was not directly retrievable during this pass (medical.edu.mt was unreachable live, and no archived tuition-specific page was found), so no official figure is asserted; a third-party aggregator (future-doctor.eu) estimates roughly EUR 19,500 per year, presented here only as an attributed third-party estimate, not an official confirmed fee -- students must confirm current tuition and payment terms directly with EDU's admissions team. Housing: EDU does not appear to operate or recommend a specific student-housing/hostel arrangement in the sources reviewed (unlike traditional campus universities), consistent with its location-independent theoretical-study model; this is stated honestly as an information gap rather than fabricated. Indian food/community: no EDU-specific or Kalkara-specific sourcing on an Indian student community or Indian food scene was found during this pass; general Malta-wide context (a modest but real Indian restaurant presence concentrated in nearby Sliema/St Julian's, a short distance from Kalkara) is used cautiously, framed as general Malta context rather than an EDU or Kalkara-specific claim. Safety: general Malta safety context (low-to-moderate crime EU country) is used rather than any EDU-specific claim; Kalkara itself is described in independent sourcing as a quiet, non-touristy harbour village.
structuredFacts.recognitionNotes: EDU's Bachelor of Medicine and Master of Medicine are accredited in Malta by the Malta Further and Higher Education Authority (MFHEA) and separately accredited by the German agency AHPGS (4 May 2023); the programme is supervised under Article 24 of EU Directive 2005/36/EC by Maastricht University's Faculty of Health, Medicine and Life Sciences since February 2020. As of the most recently available official accreditation page reviewed, automatic EU recognition via the Medical Council of Malta was still described as 'in discussion', not finalised, and EDU expected its first graduates only in 2025. No World Directory of Medical Schools (WDOMS) listing for EDU/Digital Education Holdings was found during this research pass. This page does not assert Indian NMC recognition, NEET eligibility, or FMGE/NExT status for this programme; students must verify all of these directly with NMC and with EDU before enrolling.

draftContent.summary: EDU is the medical-education brand of Digital Education Holdings Ltd (DEH), a private Institution of Higher Education licensed in Malta since 2018, and it runs what it describes as Europe's first primarily digital medical school. Rather than a traditional in-person campus programme, EDU's 5.5-year Programme in Medicine combines a 3-year Bachelor of Medicine (B.Med., 180 ECTS) and a 2.5-year Master of Medicine (M.Med., 162 ECTS): theoretical study happens through EDU's own online 'digital campus' in small tutor-mentored groups, while practical clinical training takes place in four-week rotation blocks at partner teaching hospitals based in Germany, including Helios Kliniken and Marienhaus-Gruppe sites. The programme is licensed by Malta's Further and Higher Education Authority (MFHEA), separately accredited by the German agency AHPGS, and supervised under EU Directive 2005/36/EC by Maastricht University's Faculty of Health, Medicine and Life Sciences. Two caveats matter especially for Indian applicants: clinical rotations are conducted in German, and EDU's own admissions prerequisites list a German C1 language certificate (alongside English B2) as a requirement for clinical training; and, at the time of this research, EDU was still in discussions with the Medical Council of Malta to finalise automatic EU recognition, had not yet produced its first graduating cohort (expected 2025), and no listing for EDU could be found in the World Directory of Medical Schools (WDOMS). Students must independently verify current WDOMS status, Medical Council of Malta recognition, and India's NMC/NEET/FMGE-NExT requirements before applying, since none of these is confirmed here.
draftContent.campusLifestyle: EDU's model is deliberately different from a traditional single-campus medical school: theoretical coursework is delivered through its own online 'digital campus' platform, with students learning collaboratively in small groups of up to five alongside dedicated tutors and mentors, rather than in large in-person lecture halls. Because the online component is location-independent, day-to-day academic life during theoretical trimesters can happen from wherever a student is based, with structured virtual classrooms, interactive sessions, and a defined trimester academic calendar providing the routine. The practical counterpart to this online study is intensive, in-person: from as early as Week 10 of the Bachelor, students travel to partner teaching hospitals in Germany for four-week clinical rotation blocks, several times a year, working directly with patients and clinicians under supervision. This creates a hybrid lifestyle -- extended stretches of flexible online study interspersed with concentrated, hospital-based immersion abroad -- that suits students who are self-directed and comfortable with a less conventional 'campus town' experience than a traditional MBBS destination offers.
draftContent.cityProfile: EDU/Digital Education Holdings is currently listed, in Malta's Euroguidance national schools directory, with a base in Kalkara, a small village of a little over 3,000 residents on the eastern shore of Malta's Grand Harbour, adjoining the historic 'Three Cities' (Cottonera) area and only about 4 kilometres from the capital, Valletta, and roughly 3 kilometres from Malta International Airport. Kalkara is generally described as quieter and less touristy than nearby hubs such as Sliema or St Julian's, with a picturesque fishing-village waterfront, Fort Ricasoli, and Malta's national science centre among its notable landmarks. Because EDU's theoretical study is online and location-independent, students are not necessarily required to live in Kalkara day-to-day for the coursework itself, but it is useful context as the institution's registered Malta base. English is one of Malta's two official languages, so everyday life, services, and English-medium coursework are broadly accessible; however, the clinical-training phase of this specific programme takes students to Germany, where day-to-day language needs shift to German, a distinct consideration from most Malta-based medical programmes that keep clinical training on the island itself.
draftContent.clinicalExposure: Clinical training is structured as repeated four-week rotation blocks at EDU's partner teaching hospitals in Germany, with named partners including Helios Kliniken and Marienhaus-Gruppe sites; students get their first clinical exposure from around Week 10 of the Bachelor of Medicine and continue with roughly three months of hospital-based rotation per year across the programme. The Master of Medicine concludes with a six-month Clinical Clerkship, giving students an extended, capstone period of supervised patient-facing practice before graduation. Because these rotations happen in Germany rather than in Malta, and are conducted in German, clinical training here has a genuinely different profile from Malta-campus medical schools that place students in Maltese teaching hospitals throughout: students should expect to travel to Germany repeatedly for rotation blocks and to function clinically in German, which is why EDU's own admissions prerequisites list a German C1 language certificate for clinical training as a requirement (waived only where an applicant's prior schooling was itself conducted in German). Specific hospital rotation sites, specialties, and scheduling should be confirmed directly with EDU's Faculty of Medicine and Health before enrolling.
draftContent.hostelOverview: No EDU-run or EDU-recommended student housing or hostel arrangement in Malta was found in the sources reviewed for this research, which is consistent with EDU's location-independent, primarily online theoretical-study model -- unlike a traditional single-campus university, EDU does not appear to position itself as a residential campus institution during the online-study trimesters. For the recurring clinical-rotation blocks in Germany, students should expect to need to arrange their own accommodation near each partner teaching hospital for the duration of each four-week rotation (and the six-month Clinical Clerkship in the Master's year), and should ask EDU's admissions and student-support teams directly what guidance or partner arrangements, if any, exist for this recurring housing need, since this was not itemised in the sources available during this research pass.
draftContent.indianFoodSupport: Because EDU's theoretical study is online and location-independent, and its clinical rotations take place in Germany rather than on a single Malta campus, day-to-day food access will depend heavily on wherever an individual student is based during online-study periods, and on the specific German rotation-hospital location during clinical blocks -- neither of which is fixed to one town the way a traditional campus university would be. For general Malta context (relevant to EDU's registered base near Kalkara and the wider Grand Harbour area), Malta has a modest Indian restaurant presence concentrated mainly in nearby Sliema and St Julian's, a short distance away, though nothing on the scale of larger European student hubs; this is general country-level context rather than an EDU-specific finding. Students relying on Indian staples during either the online-study phase or German clinical rotations should plan on self-catering with supermarket international-food aisles and specialty stores, and should ask EDU's student-support team and any current students for first-hand, location-specific guidance, since no EDU- or Kalkara-specific Indian food/community sourcing was found during this research pass.
draftContent.safetyOverview: Malta is broadly regarded as a safe EU destination for international students, and Kalkara -- the base associated with EDU/Digital Education Holdings in Malta's national schools directory -- is described in independent sourcing as a quiet, low-key village well away from the busier nightlife districts, making it a generally calm base within the wider safe-EU-country context. Because a meaningful part of this programme (the clinical-rotation blocks and Clerkship) takes place in Germany rather than Malta, students should also research and follow standard personal-safety and local-registration practices for each specific German host city/hospital location during those periods, in addition to any guidance for their time in Malta. As with any destination, Indian students should stay in touch with the Indian High Commission's coverage for Malta and, separately, India's diplomatic mission in Germany for consular guidance relevant to the periods spent in each country.
draftContent.studentSupport: EDU's admissions process is structured and staged -- an online application, an ITB Consulting online cognitive test, a video interview assessing motivation and soft skills, an admission decision with a follow-up call to discuss contract and financing, and finally matriculation -- with a dedicated admissions team supporting applicants through each step. The programme is also described as offering dedicated tutors and mentors supporting small learning groups throughout the online theoretical study, which is central to EDU's teaching model. For Indian applicants specifically, EDU's general prerequisites (matriculation-level secondary certificate, English B2, German C1 for clinical training, police check, CV, cover letter, valid passport/ID) should be discussed directly with EDU's admissions team to confirm exact equivalency requirements for Indian Class-XII/board qualifications, since India-specific guidance was not found as a distinct published page during this research pass. Students must independently verify current WDOMS listing status, Medical Council of Malta automatic-recognition status, and India's NMC/NEET/FMGE-NExT requirements with the relevant regulators before enrolling, since this page does not assert any of these as confirmed, and should also register with the Indian High Commission's coverage for Malta for consular support.
whyChoose: A genuinely different, technology-forward medical-education model: small-group, tutor-mentored online theoretical study combined with real, hands-on four-week clinical rotation blocks at partner teaching hospitals in Germany from as early as Week 10 of the Bachelor

External academic quality assurance from an established European medical faculty: since February 2020 the programme has been supervised under EU Directive 2005/36/EC by the Faculty of Health, Medicine and Life Sciences at Maastricht University, alongside MFHEA licensing in Malta and AHPGS accreditation (2023) from a recognised German accreditation agency

A structured, staged admissions process (application, cognitive test, interview, decision, matriculation) with defined prerequisites, giving applicants a clear and relatively transparent path to an admission decision

Location-independent theoretical study offers flexibility for students who need or prefer to manage part of their coursework remotely, combined with immersive, hospital-based clinical exposure rather than purely simulated clinical training

A young but actively evolving programme with EU-based clinical partners (Helios Kliniken, Marienhaus-Gruppe) in Germany, appealing to students specifically interested in eventually working within the German/European healthcare system
thingsToConsider: This is not a traditional single-campus MBBS: theoretical study is delivered online, and clinical training happens in four-week rotation blocks at partner hospitals in Germany rather than in Malta itself -- students should be certain this hybrid, travel-intensive model suits them before applying

Clinical rotations are conducted in German, and EDU's own stated admissions prerequisites require a German C1 language certificate for clinical training (in addition to English B2 for theoretical coursework) unless an applicant's prior schooling was itself in German -- a substantial extra language commitment beyond most English-medium MBBS-abroad options

At the time of this research, EDU was still in discussions with the Medical Council of Malta to finalise automatic EU recognition of the degree, and had not yet produced its first graduating cohort (expected 2025); this means there is very limited real-world outbound-recognition track record so far, and students should treat automatic EU recognition as not-yet-confirmed rather than settled

No listing for EDU / Digital Education Holdings was found in the World Directory of Medical Schools (WDOMS) during this research pass; since WDOMS listing is a common reference point in India's NMC framework for assessing foreign medical qualifications, students must directly verify current WDOMS status and NMC recognition before assuming this route is NMC-compliant

Official, current tuition figures could not be retrieved directly from EDU during this research pass (only a third-party estimate of roughly EUR 19,500/year was found); students must obtain and confirm the current official fee schedule and payment terms directly from EDU's admissions team before budgeting

No EDU-run or EDU-recommended student housing arrangement was found in the sources reviewed, for either the Malta-based online-study periods or the recurring German clinical-rotation blocks, so students should plan to independently arrange and budget for accommodation, including for repeated stays in Germany

As a newer institution (est. 2018) with a non-traditional delivery model, EDU has a shorter track record and a much smaller/less-documented Indian-student presence than long-established Malta or wider-Europe medical schools, so first-hand, India-specific guidance is comparatively harder to find

faq.question: Is EDU a traditional medical school with a campus, or is it fully online?
faq.answer: It is a hybrid model, not a purely online degree and not a traditional single-campus school either. EDU delivers its theoretical coursework through its own online 'digital campus' platform in small, tutor-mentored groups, but requires substantial in-person clinical training: students complete four-week clinical rotation blocks at partner teaching hospitals in Germany starting from around Week 10 of the Bachelor of Medicine, and the Master of Medicine concludes with a six-month, fully in-person Clinical Clerkship. Students should understand this combination clearly before applying, since it differs meaningfully from both fully online programmes and conventional single-campus medical schools.

faq.question: Do I need to know German to study medicine at EDU?
faq.answer: Yes, for the clinical component. Theoretical coursework is in English (B2 proficiency required), but EDU's partner teaching hospitals for clinical rotations are based in Germany and clinical training is conducted in German. EDU's own stated admissions prerequisites list a German language certificate at C1 level as a requirement for clinical training, waived only if the applicant's own prior schooling was conducted in German. Indian applicants should factor in the time and cost of reaching C1-level German alongside their medical studies.

faq.question: Will an EDU Bachelor of Medicine / Master of Medicine be recognised in India, and is EDU listed in WDOMS?
faq.answer: This is not confirmed, and students must verify it directly before enrolling. No listing for EDU or Digital Education Holdings was found in the World Directory of Medical Schools (WDOMS) during this research pass, and at the time of the most recently available official EDU accreditation page reviewed, EDU stated it was still in discussions with the Medical Council of Malta to finalise automatic EU recognition of the degree. Since India's National Medical Commission (NMC) framework for assessing foreign medical qualifications commonly references WDOMS listing, and since NMC's own approved/recognised-institution rules can change, applicants must independently confirm current WDOMS status and NMC recognition requirements -- including NEET eligibility before enrolling and the FMGE/NExT licensing exam needed to practise in India afterward -- directly with NMC before relying on this programme for a medical career in India.

faq.question: How much does EDU's Programme in Medicine cost, and what does tuition include?
faq.answer: EDU's own current, official fee schedule could not be retrieved directly during this research pass (the live site was unreachable and no archived tuition-specific page was located). A third-party study-abroad information site estimated tuition at roughly EUR 19,500 per year at time of that source's publication, but this figure is not confirmed from EDU's own current materials and should not be relied upon for budgeting. Applicants must request the current official fee schedule, payment structure, and any additional required costs (such as travel and accommodation for the recurring German clinical rotations) directly from EDU's admissions team before applying.

faq.question: Where are EDU's clinical rotations held, and where do students stay during them?
faq.answer: Clinical rotations are held at EDU's partner teaching hospitals in Germany, with named partners including Helios Kliniken and Marienhaus-Gruppe sites, in repeated four-week blocks throughout the Bachelor of Medicine and culminating in a six-month Clinical Clerkship during the Master of Medicine. No EDU-run or EDU-recommended accommodation arrangement for these recurring rotation periods was found in the sources reviewed for this research; students should ask EDU's admissions and student-support teams directly what guidance or partner housing options, if any, exist for each rotation location, and budget independently for repeated travel and stays in Germany.

faq.question: Where is EDU based in Malta, and what is that area like?
faq.answer: Malta's Euroguidance national schools directory currently lists EDU/Digital Education Holdings' base in Kalkara, a small, quiet village of a little over 3,000 residents on the eastern shore of Malta's Grand Harbour, close to the historic Three Cities area, about 4 kilometres from Valletta and roughly 3 kilometres from Malta International Airport. Because EDU's theoretical coursework is delivered online and is location-independent, students are not necessarily required to be based in Kalkara for day-to-day study, but it is useful as the institution's registered Maltese base; some of EDU's own older archived materials list a different address in nearby Gzira, so students should confirm the institution's current operating address directly with EDU.

faq.question: How competitive or established is EDU compared with other Malta medical schools?
faq.answer: EDU is a newer institution, licensed in Malta in 2018, and it expected its first Programme in Medicine graduates only in 2025 at the time of the most recent official information reviewed, so it has a much shorter track record than long-established Malta medical faculties. It has secured genuine external quality assurance -- MFHEA licensing, AHPGS accreditation in 2023, and supervision under EU Directive 2005/36/EC by Maastricht University's Faculty of Health, Medicine and Life Sciences since 2020 -- but automatic EU degree recognition via the Medical Council of Malta was still described as under discussion at that point, and no WDOMS listing was found. Applicants weighing EDU against more established Malta options should factor in this shorter history and currently less-settled recognition picture.
